summary: Problem 8 is an open research-level conjecture by Mohammed Abouzaid asking whether a combinatorial condition (exactly 4 Lagrangian faces meeting at each vertex) on a polyhedral Lagrangian surface in R^4 forces the existence of a Lagrangian smoothing. With a 9% solvability score and no substantive proof produced, the current state reflects an unsolved problem rather than a flawed proof attempt. The single tracked issue was a seed placeholder closed because there was no proof to evaluate.
problems: No actual proof attempt exists to evaluate — the 'best proof' entry shows 100 open issues and no real candidate solution.

The core mathematical blocker is foundational: there is no known general construction or obstruction theory linking the local combinatorial vertex condition (4 faces per vertex) to global existence of a Hamiltonian-isotopy smoothing.
Resolving the conjecture likely requires new symplectic-topological machinery (e.g., local Lagrangian models near singular vertices, gluing/obstruction analysis), none of which is present.
The only tracked issue was a placeholder/seed task with no specific error or technical content, so it provides no actionable lead toward a proof.
Ambiguity in the experiment/model provenance ('Experiment: ?', 'Model: claude_opus_5-4') makes it hard to assess what prior work, if any, was attempted.
highlights: The problem is clearly and rigorously posed by a leading expert, with precise definitions of polyhedral Lagrangian surfaces and Lagrangian smoothings.
The seed issue was correctly triaged and closed as a non-actionable placeholder rather than left misleadingly open.
The low solvability score (9%) is an honest signal that this is a frontier open question, setting realistic expectations for effort allocation.

suggested_todos.title: Reclassify q8 as an open research conjecture (not a solvable proof task) to avoid wasted automated proof-generation cycles
Compile a literature survey on Lagrangian smoothings of singular/polyhedral Lagrangians and local Lagrangian vertex models in R^4 to identify available techniques
Construct and document explicit low-complexity examples of 4-faces-per-vertex polyhedral Lagrangians and attempt smoothings by hand to gather evidence for/against the conjecture
Formulate a precise local model problem at a single vertex (the likely first technical step) as a tractable sub-question
Clarify and record the experiment/model provenance metadata so future attempts have an accurate baseline
